tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإذا قيل لهم اتقوا ما بين أيديكم» من عذاب الدنيا كغيرهم «وما خلفكم» من عذاب الآخرة «لعلكم ترحمون» أعرضوا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱتَّقُواْ مَا بَيۡنَ أَيۡدِيكُمۡ وَمَا خَلۡفَكُمۡ لَعَلَّكُمۡ تُرۡحَمُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan apabila dikatakan kepada mereka, "Takutlah kalian akan siksa yang di hadapan kalian) berupa azab di dunia sebagaimana apa yang telah menimpa orang-orang selain mereka (dan siksa yang akan datang) yaitu azab di akhirat (supaya kalian mendapat rahmat") tetapi mereka tetap berpaling

ar: وَمَا تَأۡتِیهِم مِّنۡ ءَایَةࣲ مِّنۡ ءَایَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمۡ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ عَنۡهَا مُعۡرِضِینَ
ar_simple: وما تأتیهم من ءایة من ءایت ربهم إلا كانوا عنها معرضین

t.sahih-int: And no sign comes to them from the signs of their Lord except that they are from it turning away
t.asad: and no message of their Sustainer s messages ever reaches them without their turning away from it
t.yusuf-ali: Not a Sign comes to them from among the Signs of their Lord, but they turn away therefrom
t.pickthall: Never came a token of the tokens of their Lord to them, but they did turn away from it
t.arberry: yet never any sign of the signs of their Lord comes to them, but they are turning away from it
t.hilali-khan: And never came an Ayah from among the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of their Lord to them, but they did turn away from it
t.itani: Yet never came to them a sign of their Lord’s signs, but they turned away from it.
t.mubarakpuri: And never came an Ayah from among the Ayat of their Lord to them, but they did turn away from it.
t.qarib: they turn away from every sign that comes to them from their Lord
t.wahiduddin: Indeed, not one of your Lord's signs comes to them without their turning away from it
t.shakir: And there comes not to them a communication of the communications of their Lord but they turn aside from it
t.haleem: they ignore every single sign that comes to them from their Lord
t.maariful: There comes to them no sign from the signs of your Lord, but they turn averse to it
t.khattab: Whenever a sign comes to them from their Lord, they turn away from it
t.ghali: And in no way does any sign of the signs of their Lord come up to them, except that they were veering away from it
t.irving: not one of their Lord´s signs has come to them except they tried to avoid it
t.iwq: And no sign comes to them from the signs of their Lord except that they are from it turning away

t_id.kemenag: Dan sekali-kali tiada datang kepada mereka suatu tanda dari tanda tanda kekuasaan Tuhan mereka, melainkan mereka selalu berpaling daripadanya.
t_id.sabiq: Tak ada satu bukti pun dari sekian banyak bukti yang mennjukkan keesaan dan kekuasaan Allah yang didatangkan kepada mereka, kecuali mereka selalu menolak bukti-bukti itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sekali-kali tiada datang kepada mereka suatu tanda dari tanda-tanda kekuasaan Rabb mereka melainkan mereka selalu berpaling daripadanya.)
t_id.iwq: Dan sekali-kali tiada datang kepada mereka suatu tanda dari tanda tanda kekuasaan Tuhan mereka, melainkan mereka selalu berpaling daripadanya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وما تأتيهم من آية من آيات ربهم إلا كانوا عنها معرضين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَا تَأۡتِيهِم مِّنۡ ءَايَةٖ مِّنۡ ءَايَٰتِ رَبِّهِمۡ إِلَّا كَانُواْ عَنۡهَا مُعۡرِضِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dan sekali-kali tiada datang kepada mereka suatu tanda dari tanda-tanda kekuasaan Rabb mereka melainkan mereka selalu berpaling daripadanya

ar: وَإِذَا قِیلَ لَهُمۡ أَنفِقُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ قَالَ ٱلَّذِینَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلَّذِینَ ءَامَنُوۤا۟ أَنُطۡعِمُ مَن لَّوۡ یَشَاۤءُ ٱللَّهُ أَطۡعَمَهُۥۤ إِنۡ أَنتُمۡ إِلَّا فِی ضَلَـٰلࣲ مُّبِینࣲ
ar_simple: وإذا قیل لهم أنفقوا مما رزقكم ٱلله قال ٱلذین كفروا للذین ءامنوا أنطعم من لو یشاء ٱلله أطعمه إن أنتم إلا فی ضلل مبین

t.sahih-int: And when it is said to them, "Spend from that which Allah has provided for you," those who disbelieve say to those who believe, "Should we feed one whom, if Allah had willed, He would have fed? You are not but in clear error
t.asad: Thus, when they are told, “Spend on others out of what God has provided for you as sustenance,” those who are bent on denying the truth say unto those who believe, “Shall we feed anyone whom, if [your] God had so willed, He could have fed [Himself]? Clearly, you are but lost in error!”
t.yusuf-ali: And when they are told, "Spend ye of (the bounties) with which Allah has provided you," the Unbelievers say to those who believe: "Shall we then feed those whom, if Allah had so willed, He would have fed, (Himself)?- Ye are in nothing but manifest error
t.pickthall: And when it is said unto them: Spend of that wherewith Allah hath provided you, those who disbelieve say unto those who believe: Shall we feed those whom Allah, if He willed, would feed? Ye are in naught else than error manifest
t.arberry: And when it is said to them, 'Expend of that God has provided you,' the unbelievers say to the believers, 'What, shall we feed such a one whom, if God willed, He would feed? You are only in manifest error
t.hilali-khan: And when it is said to them: "Spend of that with which Allah has provided you," those who disbelieve say to those who believe: "Shall we feed those whom, if Allah willed, He (Himself) would have fed? You are only in a plain error
t.itani: And when it is said to them, “Spend of what God has provided for you,” those who disbelieve say to those who believe, “Shall we feed someone whom God could feed, if He so willed? You must be deeply misguided.”
t.mubarakpuri: And when it is said to them: "Spend of that with which Allah has provided you," those who disbelieve say to those who believe: "Shall we feed those whom, if Allah willed, He would have fed You are only in a plain error."
t.qarib: And when It is said to them, "Give to others from what God has provided for you," the unbelievers say to those who believe, "Should we feed one whom, if God had willed, He would have fed? You are clearly misguided
t.wahiduddin: and when they are told, "Give to others out of what God has provided for you," those who are bent on denying the truth say to the believers, "Why should we feed those whom God could feed if He wanted? You are clearly in error
t.shakir: And when it is said to them: Spend out of what Allah has given you, those who disbelieve say to those who believe: Shall we feed him whom, if Allah please, He could feed? You are in naught but clear error
t.haleem: and when they are told, ‘Give to others out of what God has provided for you,’ the disbelievers say to the believers, ‘Why should we feed those that God could feed if He wanted? You must be deeply misguided.’
t.maariful: And when it is said to them, “Spend (to the needy) from the provision Allah has given to you”, the disbelievers say to the believers, “Shall we feed those whom Allah could have fed, if Allah so willed? You are but in open error.”
t.khattab: And when it is said to them, “Donate from what Allah has provided for you,” the disbelievers say to the believers, “Why should we feed those whom Allah could have fed if He wanted to? You are clearly astray!”
t.ghali: And when it is said to them, "Expend of what Allah has provided you, " the ones who have disbelieved have said to the ones who have believed, "Shall we feed the ones whom, if Allah decides, He will feed? Decidedly you are in nothing except evident error
t.irving: When they are (also) told: "Spend something God has provided you with," the ones who disbelieve tell those who believe: "Should we feed someone whom God would feed if He so wishes? You are quite obviously in error
t.iwq: And when it is said to them, "Spend from that which Allah has provided for you," those who disbelieve say to those who believe, "Should we feed one whom, if Allah had willed, He would have fed? You are not but in clear error

t_id.kemenag: Dan apabila dikatakakan kepada mereka: "Nafkahkanlah sebahagian dari reski yang diberikan Allah kepadamu", maka orang-orang yang kafir itu berkata kepada orang-orang yang beriman: "Apakah kami akan memberi makan kepada orang-orang yang jika Allah menghendaki tentulah Dia akan memberinya makan, tiadalah kamu melainkan dalam kesesatan yang nyata".
t_id.sabiq: Dan jika diserukan kepada mereka, "Dermakanlah sebagian rezeki yang telah Allah berikan kepada kalian itu kepada orang-orang fakir," orang-orang kafir itu akan berkata kepada orang-orang Mukmin, "Apakah kami harus memberi makan seseorang yang Allah berkehendak memberinya makan? Jika demikian, berarti kami telah menentang kehendak Allah. Wahai orang-orang yang mengajak mendermakan harta, kalian tidak lain adalah sekelompok orang yang berada dalam kesesatan yang nyata dan jauh dari kebenaran."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan apabila dikatakan) berkata sahabat-sahabat yang miskin (kepada mereka, "Nafkahkanlah) sedekahkanlah kepada kami (sebagian dari rezeki yang diberikan Allah kepada kalian") berupa harta benda (maka orang-orang yang kafir itu berkata kepada orang-orang yang beriman,) dengan nada yang sinis sebagai ejekan yang ditujukan kepada mereka, ("Apakah kami akan memberi makanan kepada orang-orang yang jika Allah menghendaki tentulah Dia akan memberinya makan) sesuai dengan keyakinan kalian itu. (Tiada lain kalian) yaitu apa yang kalian katakan kepada kami, padahal kalian mempunyai keyakinan bahwa Allah pasti memberi makan kalian (melainkan dalam kesesatan yang nyata") yakni jelas sesatnya. Ditegaskannya lafal Al Ladziina Kafaruu mengandung arti yang mendalam.
t_id.iwq: Dan apabila dikatakakan kepada mereka: "Nafkahkanlah sebahagian dari reski yang diberikan Allah kepadamu", maka orang-orang yang kafir itu berkata kepada orang-orang yang beriman: "Apakah kami akan memberi makan kepada orang-orang yang jika Allah menghendaki tentulah Dia akan memberinya makan, tiadalah kamu melainkan dalam kesesatan yang nyata".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإذا قيل» أي قال فقراء الصحابة «لهم أنفقوا» علينا «مما رزقكم الله» من الأموال «قال الذين كفروا للذين آمنوا» استهزاءً بهم «أنطعم من لو يشاء الله أطعمه» في معتقدكم هذا «إن» ما «أنتم» في قولكم لنا ذلك مع معتقدكم هذا «إلا في ضلال مبين» بيَّن وللتصريح بكفرهم موقع عظيم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمۡ أَنفِقُواْ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓاْ أَنُطۡعِمُ مَن لَّوۡ يَشَآءُ ٱللَّهُ أَطۡعَمَهُۥٓ إِنۡ أَنتُمۡ إِلَّا فِي ضَلَٰلٖ مُّبِينٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan apabila dikatakan) berkata sahabat-sahabat yang miskin (kepada mereka, "Nafkahkanlah) sedekahkanlah kepada kami (sebagian dari rezeki yang diberikan Allah kepada kalian") berupa harta benda (maka orang-orang yang kafir itu berkata kepada orang-orang yang beriman,) dengan nada yang sinis sebagai ejekan yang ditujukan kepada mereka, ("Apakah kami akan memberi makanan kepada orang-orang yang jika Allah menghendaki tentulah Dia akan memberinya makan) sesuai dengan keyakinan kalian itu. (Tiada lain kalian) yaitu apa yang kalian katakan kepada kami, padahal kalian mempunyai keyakinan bahwa Allah pasti memberi makan kalian (melainkan dalam kesesatan yang nyata") yakni jelas sesatnya. Ditegaskannya lafal Al Ladziina Kafaruu mengandung arti yang mendalam

ar: وَیَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلۡوَعۡدُ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَـٰدِقِینَ
ar_simple: ویقولون متى هذا ٱلوعد إن كنتم صدقین

t.sahih-int: And they say, "When is this promise, if you should be truthful
t.asad: And they add, “When is this promise [of resurrection] to be fulfilled? [Answer this] if you are men of truth!”
t.yusuf-ali: Further, they say, "When will this promise (come to pass), if what ye say is true
t.pickthall: And they say: When will this promise be fulfilled, if ye are truthful
t.arberry: They also say, 'When shall this promise come to pass, if you speak truly
t.hilali-khan: And they say: "When will this promise (i.e. Resurrection) be fulfilled, if you are truthful
t.itani: And they say, “When will this promise be, if you are truthful?”
t.mubarakpuri: And they say: "When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful"
t.qarib: They say, "When will this promise come about, if you are truthful
t.wahiduddin: They say, "When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful
t.shakir: And they say: When will this threat come to pass, if you are truthful
t.haleem: And they say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, if what you say is true?’
t.maariful: And they say, “When will this promise come true, if you are truthful?”
t.khattab: And they ask ˹the believers˺, “When will this threat come to pass, if what you say is true?”
t.ghali: And they say, "When will this promise be, in case you are sincere
t.irving: They say: "When will this promise occur if you are so truthful
t.iwq: And they say, "When is this promise, if you should be truthful

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka berkata: "Bilakah (terjadinya) janji ini (hari berbangkit) jika kamu adalah orang-orang yang benar?".
t_id.sabiq: Dengan nada mengejek, mereka berkata kepada orang-orang Mukmin, "Bilakah ancaman yang kalian katakan kepada kami akan terjadi, jika ancaman kalian itu memang benar?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata, "Bilakah terjadinya janji ini?) yakni hari berbangkit (jika kalian orang-orang yang benar?") mengenai apa yang kalian katakan.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka berkata: "Bilakah (terjadinya) janji ini (hari berbangkit) jika kamu adalah orang-orang yang benar?".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ويقولون متى هذا الوعد» بالبعث «إن كنتم صادقين» فيه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَٰذَا ٱلۡوَعۡدُ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَٰدِقِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata, "Bilakah terjadinya janji ini?) yakni hari berbangkit (jika kalian orang-orang yang benar?") mengenai apa yang kalian katakan

ar: مَا یَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا صَیۡحَةࣰ وَ ٰحِدَةࣰ تَأۡخُذُهُمۡ وَهُمۡ یَخِصِّمُونَ
ar_simple: ما ینظرون إلا صیحة و حدة تأخذهم وهم یخصمون

t.sahih-int: They do not await except one blast which will seize them while they are disputing
t.asad: [And they are unaware that] nothing awaits them beyond a single blast [of God’s punishment], which will overtake them while they are still arguing - [against resurrection]
t.yusuf-ali: They will not (have to) wait for aught but a single Blast: it will seize them while they are yet disputing among themselves
t.pickthall: They await but one Shout, which will surprise them while they are disputing
t.arberry: They are awaiting only for one Cry to seize them while they are yet disputing
t.hilali-khan: They await only but a single Saihah (shout, etc.), which will seize them while they are disputing
t.itani: All they can expect is a single blast, which will seize them while they feud.
t.mubarakpuri: They await only but a single Sayhah which will seize them while they are disputing!
t.qarib: All it takes is one blast that will seize them while they still arguing about it
t.wahiduddin: They must be waiting for but one single blast, which will overtake them while they are still disputing
t.shakir: They wait not for aught but a single cry which will overtake them while they yet contend with one another
t.haleem: But all they are waiting for is a single blast that will overtake them while they are still arguing with each other
t.maariful: They are looking for nothing but for a single Cry that will seize them when they will be quarrelling
t.khattab: They must be awaiting a single Blast, which will seize them while they are ˹entrenched˺ in ˹worldly˺ disputes
t.ghali: In no way are they awaiting anything except one Shout to take them (away) as they are in constant adversaries
t.irving: They need only wait for a single Blast to catch them while they are arguing away
t.iwq: And they do not await except one blast which will seize them while they are disputing

t_id.kemenag: Mereka tidak menunggu melainkan satu teriakan saja yang akan membinasakan mereka ketika mereka sedang bertengkar.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka tidak menunggu kecuali satu pekikan yang akan membinasakan mereka seketika itu juga. Mereka adalah sekelompok orang yang selalu bertikai demi urusan-urusan dunia dengan melupakan akhirat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: Allah berfirman, ("Mereka tidak menunggu) menanti-nanti (melainkan satu teriakan saja), yaitu tiupan malaikat Israfil yang pertama (yang akan membinasakan mereka ketika mereka sedang bertengkar") lafal Yakhishshimuuna pada asalnya adalah Yakhtashimuuna, kemudian harakat Ta dipindahkan kepada Kha, lalu Ta diidgamkan kepada Shad. Maksudnya, mereka dalam keadaan lalai dari kedatangan hari kiamat, disebabkan mereka sibuk dalam pertengkaran mereka, jual beli yang mereka lakukan, makan, dan minum serta kesibukan-kesibukan lainnya. Menurut qiraat yang lain lafal Yakhishshimuuna mempunyai Wazan sama dengan lafal Yadhribuuna, artinya sebagian dari mereka bertengkar dengan sebagian yang lain.
t_id.iwq: Mereka tidak menunggu melainkan satu teriakan saja yang akan membinasakan mereka ketika mereka sedang bertengkar.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: قال تعالى: «ما ينظرون» أي ينتظرون «إلا صيحة واحدة» وهي نفخة إسرافيل الأولى «تأخذهم وهم يخصِّمون» بالتشديد أصله يختصمون نقلت حركة التاء إلى الحاء وأدغمت في الصاد، أي وهم في غفلة عنها بتخاصم وتبايع وأكل وشرب وغير ذلك، وفي قراءة يخصمون كيضربون، أي يخصم بعضهم بعضا
tafsir.siraj-ar: مَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا صَيۡحَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ تَأۡخُذُهُمۡ وَهُمۡ يَخِصِّمُونَ (مَا يَنظُرُونَ: مَا يَنْتَظِرُونَ, صَيْحَةً وَاحِدَةً: هِيَ: نَفْخَةُ الفَزَعِ عِنْدَ قِيَامِ السَّاعَةِ, يَخِصِّمُونَ: يَخْتَصِمُونَ فِي شُؤُونِ حَيَاتِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Allah berfirman, ("Mereka tidak menunggu) menanti-nanti (melainkan satu teriakan saja), yaitu tiupan malaikat Israfil yang pertama (yang akan membinasakan mereka ketika mereka sedang bertengkar") lafal Yakhishshimuuna pada asalnya adalah Yakhtashimuuna, kemudian harakat Ta dipindahkan kepada Kha, lalu Ta diidgamkan kepada Shad. Maksudnya, mereka dalam keadaan lalai dari kedatangan hari kiamat, disebabkan mereka sibuk dalam pertengkaran mereka, jual beli yang mereka lakukan, makan, dan minum serta kesibukan-kesibukan lainnya. Menurut qiraat yang lain lafal Yakhishshimuuna mempunyai Wazan sama dengan lafal Yadhribuuna, artinya sebagian dari mereka bertengkar dengan sebagian yang lain

ar: فَلَا یَسۡتَطِیعُونَ تَوۡصِیَةࣰ وَلَاۤ إِلَىٰۤ أَهۡلِهِمۡ یَرۡجِعُونَ
ar_simple: فلا یستطیعون توصیة ولا إلى أهلهم یرجعون

t.sahih-int: And they will not be able [to give] any instruction, nor to their people can they return
t.asad: and so [sudden will be their end that] no testament will they be able to make, - nor to their own people will they return
t.yusuf-ali: No (chance) will they then have, by will, to dispose (of their affairs), nor to return to their own people
t.pickthall: Then they cannot make bequest, nor can they return to their own folk
t.arberry: then they will not be able to make any testament, nor will they return to their people
t.hilali-khan: Then they will not be able to make bequest, nor they will return to their family
t.itani: They will not be able to make a will, nor will they return to their families.
t.mubarakpuri: Then they will not be able to make bequest, nor they will return to their family.
t.qarib: They will not be able [to give] any testimony, nor will they return to their people
t.wahiduddin: They will have no time to make a will, nor shall they return to their own people
t.shakir: So they shall not be able to make a bequest, nor shall they return to their families
t.haleem: They will have no time to make bequests, nor will they have the chance to return to their own people
t.maariful: So they will not be able to make a bequest, nor will they return to their household
t.khattab: Then they will not be able to make a ˹last˺ will, nor can they return to their own people
t.ghali: Then they will not be able to make any testament, nor will they return to their families
t.irving: They will not even manage to draw up a will, nor will they return to their own people
t.iwq: And they will not be able to give any instruction, nor to their people can they return

t_id.kemenag: lalu mereka tidak kuasa membuat suatu wasiatpun dan tidak (pula) dapat kembali kepada keluarganya.
t_id.sabiq: Karena begitu cepatnya bencana itu diturunkan, mereka tidak dapat lagi berwasiat barang sedikit pun, dan juga tidak dapat menemui keluarga mereka lagi.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Lalu mereka tidak kuasa membuat suatu wasiat pun) tidak dapat berwasiat (dan tidak pula dapat kembali kepada keluarganya) dari pasar dan dari tempat-tempat kesibukan mereka, semuanya mati di tempatnya masing-masing.
t_id.iwq: lalu mereka tidak kuasa membuat suatu wasiatpun dan tidak (pula) dapat kembali kepada keluarganya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلا يستطيعون توصية» أي أن يوصوا «ولا إلى أهلهم يرجعون» من أسواقهم وأشغالهم بل يموتون فيها
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَا يَسۡتَطِيعُونَ تَوۡصِيَةٗ وَلَآ إِلَىٰٓ أَهۡلِهِمۡ يَرۡجِعُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Lalu mereka tidak kuasa membuat suatu wasiat pun) tidak dapat berwasiat (dan tidak pula dapat kembali kepada keluarganya) dari pasar dan dari tempat-tempat kesibukan mereka, semuanya mati di tempatnya masing-masing

ar: وَنُفِخَ فِی ٱلصُّورِ فَإِذَا هُم مِّنَ ٱلۡأَجۡدَاثِ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمۡ یَنسِلُونَ
ar_simple: ونفخ فی ٱلصور فإذا هم من ٱلأجداث إلى ربهم ینسلون

t.sahih-int: And the Horn will be blown; and at once from the graves to their Lord they will hasten
t.asad: And [then] the trumpet [of resurrection] will be blown - and lo! out of their graves towards their Sustainer will they all rush forth
t.yusuf-ali: The trumpet shall be sounded, when behold! from the sepulchres (men) will rush forth to their Lord
t.pickthall: And the trumpet is blown and lo! from the graves they hie unto their Lord
t.arberry: And the Trumpet shall be blown; then behold, they are sliding down from their tombs unto their Lord
t.hilali-khan: And the Trumpet will be blown (i.e. the second blowing) and behold! From the graves they will come out quickly to their Lord
t.itani: The Trumpet will be blown, then behold, they will rush from the tombs to their Lord.
t.mubarakpuri: And the Trumpet will be blown and behold from the graves they will come out quickly to their Lord.
t.qarib: And then the Horn will be blown, and at once they will rush out of the graves to their Lord
t.wahiduddin: The trumpet will be blown and, at once, they will rise up from their graves, and hasten to their Lord
t.shakir: And the trumpet shall be blown, when lo! from their graves they shall hasten on to their Lord
t.haleem: The Trumpet will be sounded and- lo and behold!- they will rush out to their Lord from their graves
t.maariful: And the Horn will be blown, and suddenly they will be rushing from their graves towards their Lord
t.khattab: The Trumpet will be blown ˹a second time˺, then—behold!—they will rush from the graves to their Lord
t.ghali: And the Trumpet is blown; then, only then, are they pressing down from the graves to their Lord
t.irving: The Trumpet will be blow and then they will swarm forth from their tombs to meet their Lord
t.iwq: And the Horn will be blown; and at once from the graves to their Lord they will hasten

t_id.kemenag: Dan ditiuplah sangkalala, maka tiba-tiba mereka keluar dengan segera dari kuburnya (menuju) kepada Tuhan mereka.
t_id.sabiq: Dan ditiuplah sangkakala sebagai tiupan kebangkitan. Maka mereka yang mati dari kubur bergegas menemui Allah. Sangkakala dan tiupan itu termasuk hal-hal yang hanya diketahui oleh Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ditiuplah sangkakala) yaitu tiupan yang kedua untuk membangkitkan makhluk supaya hidup kembali; jarak antara dua tiupan, yakni tiupan pertama dengan tiupan kedua lamanya empat puluh tahun (maka tiba-tiba mereka) orang-orang yang telah terkubur itu (dari kuburnya) dari tempat mereka dikubur (Keluar dengan segera menuju kepada Rabb mereka) mereka keluar dengan cepat lalu menuju kepada-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Dan ditiuplah sangkalala, maka tiba-tiba mereka keluar dengan segera dari kuburnya (menuju) kepada Tuhan mereka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ونفخ في الصور» هو قرن النفخة الثانية للبعث، وبين النفختين أربعون سنة «فإذا هم» أي المقبورين «من الأجداث» القبور «إلى ربهم ينسلون» يخرجون بسرعة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَنُفِخَ فِي ٱلصُّورِ فَإِذَا هُم مِّنَ ٱلۡأَجۡدَاثِ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمۡ يَنسِلُونَ (الصُّورِ: القَرْنِ الَّذِي يَنْفُخُ فِيهِ إِسْرَافِيلُ - عليه السلام -, الْأَجْدَاثِ: القُبُورِ, يَنسِلُونَ: يُسْرِعُونَ فيِ الخُرُوجِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ditiuplah sangkakala) yaitu tiupan yang kedua untuk membangkitkan makhluk supaya hidup kembali; jarak antara dua tiupan, yakni tiupan pertama dengan tiupan kedua lamanya empat puluh tahun (maka tiba-tiba mereka) orang-orang yang telah terkubur itu (dari kuburnya) dari tempat mereka dikubur (Keluar dengan segera menuju kepada Rabb mereka) mereka keluar dengan cepat lalu menuju kepada-Nya

ar: قَالُوا۟ یَـٰوَیۡلَنَا مَنۢ بَعَثَنَا مِن مَّرۡقَدِنَاۜۗ هَـٰذَا مَا وَعَدَ ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنُ وَصَدَقَ ٱلۡمُرۡسَلُونَ
ar_simple: قالوا یویلنا من بعثنا من مرقدنا هذا ما وعد ٱلرحمن وصدق ٱلمرسلون

t.sahih-int: They will say, "O woe to us! Who has raised us up from our sleeping place?" [The reply will be], "This is what the Most Merciful had promised, and the messengers told the truth
t.asad: They will say: “Oh, woe unto us! Who has roused us from our sleep [of death]?” [Whereupon they will be told:] “This is what the Most Gracious has promised! And His message-bearers spoke the truth!”
t.yusuf-ali: They will say: "Ah! Woe unto us! Who hath raised us up from our beds of repose?"... (A voice will say:) "This is what (Allah) Most Gracious had promised. And true was the word of the messengers
t.pickthall: Crying: Woe upon us! Who hath raised us from our place of sleep? This is that which the Beneficent did promise, and the messengers spoke truth
t.arberry: They say, 'Alas for us! Who roused us out of our sleeping place? This is what the All-merciful promised, and the Envoys spoke truly
t.hilali-khan: They will say: "Woe to us! Who has raised us up from our place of sleep." (It will be said to them): "This is what the Most Beneficent (Allah) had promised, and the Messengers spoke truth
t.itani: They will say, “Woe to us! Who resurrected us from our resting-place?” This is what the Most Gracious had promised, and the messengers have spoken the truth.”
t.mubarakpuri: They will say: "Woe to us! Who has raised us up from our place of sleep." (It will be said to them): "This is what the Most Gracious had promised, and the Messengers spoke truth!"
t.qarib: They will say, "How terrible for us! Who has raised us up from our resting-place?" [They will be told], "This is what the Merciful-to-all had promised, and the Messengers told the Truth
t.wahiduddin: Woe betide us!" they will say, "Who has roused us from our sleep?" This is what the Lord of Mercy promised: the messengers spoke the truth
t.shakir: They shall say: O woe to us! who has raised us up from our sleeping-place? This is what the Beneficent Allah promised and the apostles told the truth
t.haleem: They will say, ‘Alas for us! Who has resurrected us from our resting places?’ [They will be told], ‘This is what the Lord of Mercy promised, and the messengers told the truth.’
t.maariful: They will say, “Woe to us! Who has raised us from our sleeping place? “This is what RaHmān (the All-Merciful Allah) had promised, and the messengers had told the truth
t.khattab: They will cry, “Woe to us! Who has raised us up from our place of rest? This must be what the Most Compassionate warned us of; the messengers told the truth!”
t.ghali: They will say, "oh woe to us! Who has made us rise again from our sleeping- place?" (Literally: tying-place) "This is what The All-Merciful promised, and the Emissaries (have spoken) sincerely
t.irving: They will say: "It´s too bad for us! Whoever has raised us up from our sleeping quarters? This is what the Mercy-giving promised; the emissaries have been telling the truth
t.iwq: They will say, "O woe to us! Who has raised us up from our sleeping place?" The reply will be, "This is what the Ever-Merciful had promised, and the messengers told the truth

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berkata: "Aduhai celakalah kami! Siapakah yang membangkitkan kami dari tempat-tidur kami (kubur)?". Inilah yang dijanjikan (Tuhan) Yang Maha Pemurah dan benarlah Rasul-rasul(Nya).
t_id.sabiq: Mereka yang dibangkitkan dari kubur itu pun berkata, "Alangkah seramnya apa yang menanti kami ini! Siapakah gerangan yang telah membangunkan kami dari tidur ini?" Lalu datanglah jawaban atas pertanyaan mereka itu, "Inilah hari kebangkitan yang dijanjikan Sang Maha Pemurah kepada hamba-hamba-Nya. Dan benarlah para rasul yang telah menyampaikan berita tentang hal ini."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata) orang-orang kafir di antara manusia, ("Aduhai!) Ya di sini menunjukkan makna Tanbih (celakalah kami) binasalah kami lafal Wailun adalah Mashdar yang tidak mempunyai Fi'il dari lafalnya. (Siapakah yang membangkitkan kami dari tempat tidur kami -kubur-?") karena mereka seolah-olah dalam keadaan tidur di antara kedua tiupan itu, maksudnya mereka tidak diazab. (Inilah) kebangkitan ini (yang) telah (dijanjikan yang Maha Pemurah dan benarlah) mengenainya (Rasul-rasul-Nya) mereka mengakui atas kebenaran yang telah dikatakan oleh para rasul, tetapi pengakuan mereka tidak bermanfaat lagi. Menurut pendapat yang lain, bahwa kalimat tersebut dikatakan kepada mereka.
t_id.iwq: Mereka berkata: "Aduhai celakalah kami! Siapakah yang membangkitkan kami dari tempat-tidur kami (kubur)?". Inilah yang dijanjikan (Tuhan) Yang Maha Pemurah dan benarlah Rasul-rasul(Nya).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا» أي الكفار منهم «يا» للتنبيه «ويلنا» هلاكنا وهو مصدر لا فعل له من لفظه «من بعثنا من مرقدنا» لأنهم كانوا بين النفختين نائمين لم يعذبوا «هذا» أي البعث «ما» أي الذي «وعد» به «الرحمن وصدق» فيه «المرسلون» أقروا حين لا ينفعهم الإقرار، وقيل: يقال لهم ذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ يَٰوَيۡلَنَا مَنۢ بَعَثَنَا مِن مَّرۡقَدِنَاۜۗ هَٰذَا مَا وَعَدَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ وَصَدَقَ ٱلۡمُرۡسَلُونَ (مَّرْقَدِنَا: قُبُورِنَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata) orang-orang kafir di antara manusia, ("Aduhai!) Ya di sini menunjukkan makna Tanbih (celakalah kami) binasalah kami lafal Wailun adalah Mashdar yang tidak mempunyai Fi'il dari lafalnya. (Siapakah yang membangkitkan kami dari tempat tidur kami -kubur-?") karena mereka seolah-olah dalam keadaan tidur di antara kedua tiupan itu, maksudnya mereka tidak diazab. (Inilah) kebangkitan ini (yang) telah (dijanjikan yang Maha Pemurah dan benarlah) mengenainya (Rasul-rasul-Nya) mereka mengakui atas kebenaran yang telah dikatakan oleh para rasul, tetapi pengakuan mereka tidak bermanfaat lagi. Menurut pendapat yang lain, bahwa kalimat tersebut dikatakan kepada mereka

ar: إِن كَانَتۡ إِلَّا صَیۡحَةࣰ وَ ٰحِدَةࣰ فَإِذَا هُمۡ جَمِیعࣱ لَّدَیۡنَا مُحۡضَرُونَ
ar_simple: إن كانت إلا صیحة و حدة فإذا هم جمیع لدینا محضرون

t.sahih-int: It will not be but one blast, and at once they are all brought present before Us
t.asad: Nothing will there have been but one single blast - and lo! before Us will all of them be arraigned [and be told]
t.yusuf-ali: It will be no more than a single Blast, when lo! they will all be brought up before Us
t.pickthall: It is but one Shout, and behold them brought together before Us
t.arberry: It was only one Cry; then behold, they are all arraigned before Us
t.hilali-khan: It will be but a single Saihah (shout, etc.), so behold! They will all be brought up before Us
t.itani: It will be but a single scream; and behold, they will all be brought before Us.
t.mubarakpuri: It will be but a single Sayhah, so behold they will all be brought up before Us!
t.qarib: It will be only one blast, and at once they are all brought before Us
t.wahiduddin: It will be but one blast, and they will all be brought before Us together
t.shakir: There would be naught but a single cry, when lo! they shall all be brought before Us
t.haleem: It was just one single blast and then- lo and behold!- they were all brought before Us
t.maariful: It will be no more than a single Cry, and in no time they will all be arraigned before Us
t.khattab: It will only take one Blast, then at once they will all be brought before Us
t.ghali: Decidedly it was only one Shout; then, only then, will they together be closely presented to Us
t.irving: There will be only a single Blast and then they will all be assembled in Our presence
t.iwq: It will not be but one blast, and at once they are all brought present before Us

t_id.kemenag: Tidak adalah teriakan itu selain sekali teriakan saja, maka tiba-tiba mereka semua dikumpulkan kepada Kami.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka diseru untuk keluar hanya dengan satu panggilan saja. Seketika itu juga mereka pun berkumpul di hadapan Kami untuk mendapatkan perhitungan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tiadalah teriakan itu selain sekali teriakan saja, tiba-tiba mereka semua kepada Kami) di hadapan Kami (dikumpulkan.)
t_id.iwq: Tidak adalah teriakan itu selain sekali teriakan saja, maka tiba-tiba mereka semua dikumpulkan kepada Kami.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن» ما «كانت إلا صيحة واحدة فإذا هم جميع لدينا» عندنا «محضرون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِن كَانَتۡ إِلَّا صَيۡحَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ فَإِذَا هُمۡ جَمِيعٞ لَّدَيۡنَا مُحۡضَرُونَ (صَيْحَةً وَاحِدَةً: نَفْخَةً وَاحِدَةً فِي القَرْنِ, مُحْضَرُونَ: مَاثِلُونُ لِلْحِسَابِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tiadalah teriakan itu selain sekali teriakan saja, tiba-tiba mereka semua kepada Kami) di hadapan Kami (dikumpulkan)

ar: فَٱلۡیَوۡمَ لَا تُظۡلَمُ نَفۡسࣱ شَیۡءࣰا وَلَا تُجۡزَوۡنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ
ar_simple: فٱلیوم لا تظلم نفس شیءا ولا تجزون إلا ما كنتم تعملون

t.sahih-int: So today no soul will be wronged at all, and you will not be recompensed except for what you used to do
t.asad: “Today, then, no human being shall be wronged in the least, nor shalt you be requited for aught but what you were doing [on earth]
t.yusuf-ali: Then, on that Day, not a soul will be wronged in the least, and ye shall but be repaid the meeds of your past Deeds
t.pickthall: This day no soul is wronged in aught; nor are ye requited aught save what ye used to do
t.arberry: So today no soul shall be wronged anything, and you shall not be recompensed, except according to what you have been doing
t.hilali-khan: This Day (Day of Resurrection), none will be wronged in anything, nor will you be requited anything except that which you used to do
t.itani: On that Day, no soul will be wronged in the least, and you will be recompensed only for what you used to do.
t.mubarakpuri: This Day, none will be wronged in anything, nor will you be requited anything except that which you used to do.
t.qarib: Today no soul will be wronged at all, and you will be repaid for what you did
t.wahiduddin: On that Day no soul shall suffer the least injustice. You shall be rewarded only according to your deeds
t.shakir: So this day no soul shall be dealt with unjustly in the least; and you shall not be rewarded aught but that which you did
t.haleem: ‘Today, no soul will be wronged in the least: you will only be repaid for your deeds
t.maariful: Then, nobody will be subjected to injustice in the least, and you will not be recompensed but for what you used to do
t.khattab: On that Day no soul will be wronged in the least, nor will you be rewarded except for what you used to do
t.ghali: So today no self will be unjustly (treated) in anything, and you will not be recompensed, except (for) whatever you were doing
t.irving: On that day no soul will be harmed in any way, and you will be rewarded only for what you have been doing
t.iwq: Thus today no soul will be wronged at all, and you will not be recompensed except for what you used to do

t_id.kemenag: Maka pada hari itu seseorang tidak akan dirugikan sedikitpun dan kamu tidak dibalasi, kecuali dengan apa yang telah kamu kerjakan.
t_id.sabiq: Maka pada hari itu, seseorang tidak akan dicurangi balasannya sesuai dengan perbuatannya. Mereka tidak akan mendapatkan sesuatu kecuali balasan atas perbuatan baik atau buruk yang telah mereka lakukan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Pada hari itu seseorang tidak akan dirugikan sedikit pun dan kalian tidak dibalasi, kecuali) dengan balasan (apa yang telah kalian kerjakan.)
t_id.iwq: Maka pada hari itu seseorang tidak akan dirugikan sedikitpun dan kamu tidak dibalasi, kecuali dengan apa yang telah kamu kerjakan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فاليوم لا تظلم نفسٌ شيئا ولا تجزون إلا» جزاء «ما كنتم تعملون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَٱلۡيَوۡمَ لَا تُظۡلَمُ نَفۡسٞ شَيۡـٔٗا وَلَا تُجۡزَوۡنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pada hari itu seseorang tidak akan dirugikan sedikit pun dan kalian tidak dibalasi, kecuali) dengan balasan (apa yang telah kalian kerjakan)

ar: إِنَّ أَصۡحَـٰبَ ٱلۡجَنَّةِ ٱلۡیَوۡمَ فِی شُغُلࣲ فَـٰكِهُونَ
ar_simple: إن أصحب ٱلجنة ٱلیوم فی شغل فكهون

t.sahih-int: Indeed the companions of Paradise, that Day, will be amused in [joyful] occupation
t.asad: “Behold, those who are destined for paradise shall today have joy in whatever they do
t.yusuf-ali: Verily the Companions of the Garden shall that Day have joy in all that they do
t.pickthall: Lo! those who merit paradise this day are happily employed
t.arberry: See, the inhabitants of Paradise today are busy in their rejoicing
t.hilali-khan: Verily, the dwellers of the Paradise, that Day, will be busy in joyful things
t.itani: The inhabitants of Paradise, on that Day, will be happily busy.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, the dwellers of the Paradise, that Day, will be busy with joyful things.
t.qarib: The people of Paradise will on that Day be joyfully engaged
t.wahiduddin: The people of Paradise shall be happily occupied on that Day
t.shakir: Surely the dwellers of the garden shall on that day be in an occupation quite happy
t.haleem: The people of Paradise today are happily occupied
t.maariful: The people of the Paradise are engaged today in (their) activities, happily enjoying (them)
t.khattab: Indeed, on that Day the residents of Paradise will be busy enjoying themselves
t.ghali: Surely the Companions (i.e., inhabitants) of the Garden today are cheerful in their occupation
t.irving: The inhabitants of the Garden will be happily at work of that day
t.iwq: Indeed the companions of Paradise, that Day, will be amused in joyful occupation

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya penghuni surga pada hari itu bersenang-senang dalam kesibukan (mereka).
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya pada hari ini para penghuni surga disibukkan oleh kenikmatan yang mereka dapatkan. Mereka mengagumi semua itu dengan perasaan riang gembira.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya penghuni surga pada hari itu dalam kesibukan) mereka tidak menghiraukan lagi apa yang dialami oleh ahli neraka, karena mereka sibuk dengan kenikmatan-kenikmatan yang sedang mereka rasakan, seperti memecahkan selaput dara bidadari-bidadari; mereka tidak mempunyai kesibukan yang membuat mereka lelah atau payah, karena di dalam surga tidak ada kelelahan. Lafal Syughulin dapat pula dibaca Syughlin (bersenang-senang) yakni bergelimangan di dalam kenikmatan. Lafal Faakihuuna menjadi Khabar kedua dari Inna, sedangkan Khabar yang pertama adalah Fii Syughulin.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya penghuni surga pada hari itu bersenang-senang dalam kesibukan (mereka).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن أصحاب الجنة اليوم في شغْل» بسكون الغين وضمها عما فيه أهل النار مما يتلذذون به كافتضاض الأبكار، لا شغل يتعبون فيه، لأن الجنة لا نصب فيها «فاكهون» ناعمون خبر ثان لإن، والأول في شغل
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ أَصۡحَٰبَ ٱلۡجَنَّةِ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ فِي شُغُلٖ فَٰكِهُونَ (فِي شُغُلٍ: مَشْغُولُونَ بِالنَّعِيمِ عَمَّا سِوَاهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya penghuni surga pada hari itu dalam kesibukan) mereka tidak menghiraukan lagi apa yang dialami oleh ahli neraka, karena mereka sibuk dengan kenikmatan-kenikmatan yang sedang mereka rasakan, seperti memecahkan selaput dara bidadari-bidadari; mereka tidak mempunyai kesibukan yang membuat mereka lelah atau payah, karena di dalam surga tidak ada kelelahan. Lafal Syughulin dapat pula dibaca Syughlin (bersenang-senang) yakni bergelimangan di dalam kenikmatan. Lafal Faakihuuna menjadi Khabar kedua dari Inna, sedangkan Khabar yang pertama adalah Fii Syughulin

ar: هُمۡ وَأَزۡوَ ٰجُهُمۡ فِی ظِلَـٰلٍ عَلَى ٱلۡأَرَاۤئِكِ مُتَّكِءُونَ
ar_simple: هم وأزو جهم فی ظلل على ٱلأرائك متكءون

t.sahih-int: They and their spouses - in shade, reclining on adorned couches
t.asad: in happiness will they and their spouses on couches recline
t.yusuf-ali: They and their associates will be in groves of (cool) shade, reclining on Thrones (of dignity)
t.pickthall: They and their wives, in pleasant shade, on thrones reclining
t.arberry: they and their spouses, reclining upon couches in the shade
t.hilali-khan: They and their wives will be in pleasant shade, reclining on thrones
t.itani: They and their spouses, in shades, reclining on couches.
t.mubarakpuri: They and their wives will be in pleasant shade, reclining on thrones.
t.qarib: they and their spouses will lie in shades, reclining on couches
t.wahiduddin: they and their wives, shall recline on couches in the shade
t.shakir: They and their wives shall be in shades, reclining on raised couches
t.haleem: they and their spouses- seated on couches in the shade
t.maariful: They and their spouses are in pleasant shades, reclining on couches
t.khattab: They and their spouses will be in ˹cool˺ shade, reclining on ˹canopied˺ couches
t.ghali: They and their spouses are reclining upon couches in the shades
t.irving: They and their spouses will relax on couches in shady nooks
t.iwq: And they and their spouses - in shade, reclining on adorned couches

t_id.kemenag: Mereka dan isteri-isteri mereka berada dalam tempat yang teduh, bertelekan di atas dipan-dipan.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka beserta istri-istri mereka berada dalam keteduhan yang menyenangkan sambil bertelekan di atas dipan-dipan yang penuh hiasan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka) lafal Hum menjadi Mubtada (dan istri-istri mereka berada dalam tempat yang teduh) lafal Zhilaalun ini adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Zhillun atau Zhillatun; menjadi Khabar Mubtada; arti Zhillun adalah tidak terkena panas matahari maksudnya teduh. (Di atas dipan-dipan) lafal Araa-iki adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Ariikah, adalah ranjang atau permadani yang tebal (mereka bersandaran) bertelekan di atas dipan-dipan; lafal ayat ini menjadi Khabar kedua dan menjadi tempat berta'alluqnya Alal Araaa-iki.
t_id.iwq: Mereka dan isteri-isteri mereka berada dalam tempat yang teduh, bertelekan di atas dipan-dipan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «هم» مبتدأ «وأزواجهم في ظلال» جمع ظلة أو ظل خبر أي لا تصيبهم الشمس «على الأرائك» جمع أريكة، وهو السرير في الحجلة أو الفرش فيها «متكئون» خبر ثان متعلق بعلى
tafsir.siraj-ar: هُمۡ وَأَزۡوَٰجُهُمۡ فِي ظِلَٰلٍ عَلَى ٱلۡأَرَآئِكِ مُتَّكِـُٔونَ (الْأَرَائِكِ: الأَسِرَّةِ المُزَيَّنَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka) lafal Hum menjadi Mubtada (dan istri-istri mereka berada dalam tempat yang teduh) lafal Zhilaalun ini adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Zhillun atau Zhillatun; menjadi Khabar Mubtada; arti Zhillun adalah tidak terkena panas matahari maksudnya teduh. (Di atas dipan-dipan) lafal Araa-iki adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Ariikah, adalah ranjang atau permadani yang tebal (mereka bersandaran) bertelekan di atas dipan-dipan; lafal ayat ini menjadi Khabar kedua dan menjadi tempat berta'alluqnya Alal Araaa-iki

ar: لَهُمۡ فِیهَا فَـٰكِهَةࣱ وَلَهُم مَّا یَدَّعُونَ
ar_simple: لهم فیها فكهة ولهم ما یدعون

t.sahih-int: For them therein is fruit, and for them is whatever they request [or wish]
t.asad: [only] delight will there be for them, and theirs shall be all that they could ask for
t.yusuf-ali: (Every) fruit (enjoyment) will be there for them; they shall have whatever they call for
t.pickthall: Theirs the fruit (of their good deeds) and theirs (all) that they ask
t.arberry: therein they have fruits, and they have all that they call for
t.hilali-khan: They will have therein fruits (of all kinds) and all that they ask for
t.itani: They will have therein fruits. They will have whatever they call for.
t.mubarakpuri: They will have therein fruits and all that they ask for.
t.qarib: where they will have fruit and whatever they wish
t.wahiduddin: They shall have fruits therein, and all that they ask for
t.shakir: They shall have fruits therein, and they shall have whatever they desire
t.haleem: There they have fruit and whatever they ask for
t.maariful: For them there are fruits, and for them there is whatever they ask for
t.khattab: There they will have fruits and whatever they desire
t.ghali: Therein they have (every kind of) fruit, and they have whatever they claim
t.irving: they will have fruit and they shall have anything they request
t.iwq: For them therein is fruit, and for them is whatever they request or wish

t_id.kemenag: Di surga itu mereka memperoleh buah-buahan dan memperoleh apa yang mereka minta.
t_id.sabiq: Di surga itu mereka memperoleh aneka ragam buah-buahan. Semua yang mereka minta akan disediakan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Di surga itu mereka memperoleh buah-buahan dan mereka memperoleh pula) di dalamnya (apa yang mereka minta) apa yang mereka dambakan.
t_id.iwq: Di surga itu mereka memperoleh buah-buahan dan memperoleh apa yang mereka minta.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «لهم فيها فاكهة ولهم» فيها «ما يدَّعون» يتمنون
tafsir.siraj-ar: لَهُمۡ فِيهَا فَٰكِهَةٞ وَلَهُم مَّا يَدَّعُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Di surga itu mereka memperoleh buah-buahan dan mereka memperoleh pula) di dalamnya (apa yang mereka minta) apa yang mereka dambakan

ar: سَلَـٰمࣱ قَوۡلࣰا مِّن رَّبࣲّ رَّحِیمࣲ
ar_simple: سلم قولا من رب رحیم

t.sahih-int: [And] "Peace," a word from a Merciful Lord
t.asad: peace and fulfillment through the word of a Sustainer who dispenses all grace
t.yusuf-ali: Peace!" - a word (of salutation) from a Lord Most Merciful
t.pickthall: The word from a Merciful Lord (for them) is: Peace
t.arberry: Peace!' -- such is the greeting, from a Lord All-compassionate
t.hilali-khan: (It will be said to them): Salamun (peace be on you), a Word from the Lord (Allah), Most Merciful
t.itani: Peace—a saying from a Most Merciful Lord.
t.mubarakpuri: (It will be said to them): "Salam (Peace!)" -- a Word from the Lord, Most Merciful.
t.qarib: and their Lord, the Mercy giver, will great them with "Peace
t.wahiduddin: Peace!" shall be the greeting from the Merciful Lord
t.shakir: Peace: a word from a Merciful Lord
t.haleem: “Peace,” a word from the Lord of Mercy
t.maariful: “Salām ”(Peace upon you) is the word (they receive) from Merciful Lord
t.khattab: And “Peace!” will be ˹their˺ greeting from the Merciful Lord
t.ghali: Peace!" Such is the Saying from an Ever-Merciful Lord
t.irving: Peace!" will be a greeting from a Merciful Lord
t.iwq: And "Peace," a word from a Merciful Lord

t_id.kemenag: (Kepada mereka dikatakan): "Salam", sebagai ucapan selamat dari Tuhan Yang Maha Penyayang.
t_id.sabiq: Diucapkan untuk mereka ucapan keselamatan yang berasal dari Tuhan Yang Maha Pengasih.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kepada mereka dikatakan, "Salaam") kedudukan kalimat ini menjadi Mubtada (sebagai ucapan selamat) yang menjadi Khabarnya ialah (dari Rabb Yang Maha Penyayang) kepada mereka, yakni Dia mengucapkan kepada mereka, "Kesejahteraan atas kalian."
t_id.iwq: (Kepada mereka dikatakan): "Salam", sebagai ucapan selamat dari Tuhan Yang Maha Penyayang.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «سلام» مبتدأ «قولا» أي بالقول خبره «من رب رحيم» بهم، أي يقول لهم: سلام عليكم
tafsir.siraj-ar: سَلَٰمٞ قَوۡلٗا مِّن رَّبّٖ رَّحِيمٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kepada mereka dikatakan, "Salaam") kedudukan kalimat ini menjadi Mubtada (sebagai ucapan selamat) yang menjadi Khabarnya ialah (dari Rabb Yang Maha Penyayang) kepada mereka, yakni Dia mengucapkan kepada mereka, "Kesejahteraan atas kalian

ar: وَٱمۡتَـٰزُوا۟ ٱلۡیَوۡمَ أَیُّهَا ٱلۡمُجۡرِمُونَ
ar_simple: وٱمتزوا ٱلیوم أیها ٱلمجرمون

t.sahih-int: [Then He will say], "But stand apart today, you criminals
t.asad: “But stand aside today, O you who were lost in sin
t.yusuf-ali: And O ye in sin! Get ye apart this Day
t.pickthall: But avaunt ye, O ye guilty, this day
t.arberry: Now keep yourselves apart, you sinners, upon this day
t.hilali-khan: (It will be said): "And O you Al-Mujrimun (criminals, polytheists, sinners, disbelievers in the Islamic Monotheism, wicked evil ones, etc.)! Get you apart this Day (from the believers)
t.itani: But step aside today, you criminals.
t.mubarakpuri: (It will be said): "And O you the criminals! Get you apart this Day."
t.qarib: [But to the guilty, He will say], "Those who forced others to reject Our messages should stand apart today
t.wahiduddin: [And God will say], "Separate yourselves from the righteous this Day, you criminals
t.shakir: And get aside today, O guilty ones
t.haleem: But step aside today, you guilty ones
t.maariful: And (it will be said to the infidels,) “Get apart (from the believers) today O the guilty ones
t.khattab: ˹Then the disbelievers will be told,˺ “Step away ˹from the believers˺ this Day, O wicked ones
t.ghali: And keep yourselves discriminated (apart), you criminals, upon this Day
t.irving: Step aside today, you criminals
t.iwq: Then He will say, "But stand apart today, you criminals

t_id.kemenag: Dan (dikatakan kepada orang-orang kafir): "Berpisahlah kamu (dari orang-orang mukmin) pada hari ini, hai orang-orang yang berbuat jahat.
t_id.sabiq: Dan bagi para pelaku kejahatan, pada hari ini dikatakan kepada mereka, "Menyingkirlah kalian dari kelompok orang-orang yang beriman!
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan) Dia berfirman pula, ("Berpisahlah kalian dan orang-orang mukmin pada hari ini hai orang-orang yang berbuat jahat) mereka diperintahkan supaya berpisah di kala mereka bercampur dengan orang-orang mukmin.
t_id.iwq: Dan (dikatakan kepada orang-orang kafir): "Berpisahlah kamu (dari orang-orang mukmin) pada hari ini, hai orang-orang yang berbuat jahat.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «و» يقول «امتازوا اليوم أيها المجرمون» أي انفردوا عن المؤمنين عند اختلاطهم بهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱمۡتَٰزُواْ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ أَيُّهَا ٱلۡمُجۡرِمُونَ (وَامْتَازُوا: تَمَيَّزُوا وَانْفَصِلُوا عَنِ المُؤْمِنِينَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan) Dia berfirman pula, ("Berpisahlah kalian dan orang-orang mukmin pada hari ini hai orang-orang yang berbuat jahat) mereka diperintahkan supaya berpisah di kala mereka bercampur dengan orang-orang mukmin

ar: ۞ أَلَمۡ أَعۡهَدۡ إِلَیۡكُمۡ یَـٰبَنِیۤ ءَادَمَ أَن لَّا تَعۡبُدُوا۟ ٱلشَّیۡطَـٰنَۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمۡ عَدُوࣱّ مُّبِینࣱ
ar_simple: ألم أعهد إلیكم یبنی ءادم أن لا تعبدوا ٱلشیطن إنه لكم عدو مبین

t.sahih-int: Did I not enjoin upon you, O children of Adam, that you not worship Satan - [for] indeed, he is to you a clear enemy
t.asad: Did I not enjoin on you, O you children of Adam, that you should not worship Satan – since, verily, he is your open foe
t.yusuf-ali: Did I not enjoin on you, O ye Children of Adam, that ye should not worship Satan; for that he was to you an enemy avowed
t.pickthall: Did I not charge you, O ye sons of Adam, that ye worship not the devil - Lo! he is your open foe
t.arberry: Made I not covenant with you, Children of Adam, that you should not serve Satan -- surely he is a manifest foe to you
t.hilali-khan: Did I not ordain for you, O Children of Adam, that you should not worship Shaitan (Satan). Verily, he is a plain enemy to you
t.itani: Did I not covenant with you, O Children of Adam, that you shall not serve the devil? That he is your sworn enemy?
t.mubarakpuri: "Did I not command you, O Children of Adam, that you should not worship Shaytan Verily, he is a plain enemy to you."
t.qarib: Children of Adam, did I not make a covenant with you not to worship Satan, who is clearly your enemy
t.wahiduddin: Did I not enjoin you, sons of Adam, not to worship Satan -- for he is your sworn enemy
t.shakir: Did I not charge you, O children of Adam! that you should not serve the Shaitan? Surely he is your open enemy
t.haleem: Children of Adam, did I not command you not to serve Satan, for he was your sworn enemy
t.maariful: Did I not direct you, O children of ’Ādam (Adam), that you must not worship the Satan, (because) he is an open enemy for you
t.khattab: Did I not command you, O Children of Adam, not to follow Satan, for he is truly your sworn enemy
t.ghali: Did I not covenant you, O you Seeds (Or: sons) of Adam, that you should not worship Ash-Shaytan (The Prophet) __ surely he is an evident enemy to you
t.irving: Did I not contract you, O children of Adam, not to serve Satan? He is an open enemy of yours
t.iwq: Did I not enjoin upon you, O children of Adam, that you not worship Satan - for indeed, he is to you a clear enemy

t_id.kemenag: Bukankah Aku telah memerintahkan kepadamu hai Bani Adam supaya kamu tidak menyembah syaitan? Sesungguhnya syaitan itu adalah musuh yang nyata bagi kamu",
t_id.sabiq: Bukankah telah Aku wasiatkan kepada kalian, wahai anak keturunan Adam, agar kalian tidak mengikuti jalan setan sehingga kalian menjadi budaknya? Sesungguhnya setan itu merupakan musuh yang nyata.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Bukankah Aku telah memerintahkan kepada kalian hai Bani Adam) melalui lisan Rasul-rasul-Ku (supaya kalian tidak menyembah setan) jangan menaatinya. (Sesungguhnya setan itu adalah musuh yang nyata bagi kalian") yakni jelas permusuhannya.
t_id.iwq: Bukankah Aku telah memerintahkan kepadamu hai Bani Adam supaya kamu tidak menyembah syaitan? Sesungguhnya syaitan itu adalah musuh yang nyata bagi kamu",

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ألم أعهد إليكم» آمركم «يابني آدم» على لسان رسلي «أن لا تعبدوا الشيطان» لا تطيعوه «إنه لكم عدوٌ مبين» بيَّن العداوة
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞أَلَمۡ أَعۡهَدۡ إِلَيۡكُمۡ يَٰبَنِيٓ ءَادَمَ أَن لَّا تَعۡبُدُواْ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنَۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمۡ عَدُوّٞ مُّبِينٞ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Bukankah Aku telah memerintahkan kepada kalian hai Bani Adam) melalui lisan Rasul-rasul-Ku (supaya kalian tidak menyembah setan) jangan menaatinya. (Sesungguhnya setan itu adalah musuh yang nyata bagi kalian") yakni jelas permusuhannya

ar: وَأَنِ ٱعۡبُدُونِیۚ هَـٰذَا صِرَ ٰطࣱ مُّسۡتَقِیمࣱ
ar_simple: وأن ٱعبدونی هذا صر ط مستقیم

t.sahih-int: And that you worship [only] Me? This is a straight path
t.asad: and that you should worship Me [alone]? This would have been a straight way
t.yusuf-ali: And that ye should worship Me, (for that) this was the Straight Way
t.pickthall: But that ye worship Me? That was the right path
t.arberry: and that you should serve Me? This is a straight path
t.hilali-khan: And that you should worship Me [Alone Islamic Monotheism, and set up not rivals, associate-gods with Me]. That is a Straight Path
t.itani: And that you shall serve Me? This is a straight path.
t.mubarakpuri: "And that you should worship Me. That is the straight path."
t.qarib: and that you worship [only] Me? This is a straight path
t.wahiduddin: but to worship Me? Surely, that is a straight path
t.shakir: And that you should serve Me; this is the right way
t.haleem: but to serve Me? This is the straight path
t.maariful: and that you must worship Me, (because) this is the straight path
t.khattab: but to worship Me ˹alone˺? This is the Straight Path
t.ghali: And that you should worship Me? This is a straight Path
t.irving: And to worship Me [Alone]! This is a Straight Road
t.iwq: And that you worship only Me? This is a straight path

t_id.kemenag: dan hendaklah kamu menyembah-Ku. Inilah jalan yang lurus.
t_id.sabiq: Juga agar kalian mengesakan Aku dalam beribadah, karena itu merupakan jalan yang benar-benar lurus.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan hendaklah kalian menyembah-Ku) yakni esakanlah Aku dan taatilah Aku. (Inilah jalan) maksudnya tuntunan (yang lurus.)
t_id.iwq: dan hendaklah kamu menyembah-Ku. Inilah jalan yang lurus.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وأن اعبدوني» وحِّدوني وأطيعوني «هذا صراط» طريق «مستقيم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَأَنِ ٱعۡبُدُونِيۚ هَٰذَا صِرَٰطٞ مُّسۡتَقِيمٞ (هَذَا صِرَاطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ: عِبَادَتِي وَمَعْصِيَةُ الشَّيْطَانِ طَرِيقٌ قَوِيمٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan hendaklah kalian menyembah-Ku) yakni esakanlah Aku dan taatilah Aku. (Inilah jalan) maksudnya tuntunan (yang lurus)

ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَضَلَّ مِنكُمۡ جِبِلࣰّا كَثِیرًاۖ أَفَلَمۡ تَكُونُوا۟ تَعۡقِلُونَ
ar_simple: ولقد أضل منكم جبلا كثیرا أفلم تكونوا تعقلون

t.sahih-int: And he had already led astray from among you much of creation, so did you not use reason
t.asad: And [as for Satan -] he had already led astray a great many of you: could you not, then, use your reason
t.yusuf-ali: But he did lead astray a great multitude of you. Did ye not, then, understand
t.pickthall: Yet he hath led astray of you a great multitude. Had ye then no sense
t.arberry: He led astray many a throng of you; did you not understand
t.hilali-khan: And indeed he (Satan) did lead astray a great multitude of you. Did you not, then, understand
t.itani: He has misled a great multitude of you. Did you not understand?
t.mubarakpuri: "And indeed he did lead astray a great multitude of you. Did you not then understand"
t.qarib: [Satan] has already led a great many of you astray; why do you not use your reason
t.wahiduddin: Yet he led astray a great multitude of you. Why did you not then understand
t.shakir: And certainly he led astray numerous people from among you. What! could you not then understand
t.haleem: He has led great numbers of you astray. Did you not use your reason
t.maariful: He had misguided lot many people from among you. So, did you not have sense
t.khattab: Yet he already misled great multitudes of you. Did you not have any sense
t.ghali: And indeed he has led into error many a crowd of you. Did you not then consider
t.irving: He has led a numerous mob of you astray. Haven´t you been reasoning things out
t.iwq: And he had already led astray from among you much of creation, so did you not use reason

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya syaitan itu telah menyesatkan sebahagian besar diantaramu, Maka apakah kamu tidak memikirkan?.
t_id.sabiq: Sungguh setan telah menyesatkan banyak di antara kalian. Kalian telah tertipu mengapa tidak berpikir saat mengikuti jalan setan."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya setan itu telah menyesatkan sebagian besar di antara kalian) lafal Jibillan adalah bentuk jamak dari Jabiilun seperti wazan Qadiimun, artinya makhluk. Menurut qiraat yang lain dibaca Jibullan dengan harakat Dhammah pada huruf Ba. (Maka apakah kalian tidak memikirkan?) tentang permusuhan setan dan penyesatannya; atau azab yang bakal menimpa mereka, yang karenanya mereka lalu mau beriman. Dikatakan kepada mereka di akhirat nanti:
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya syaitan itu telah menyesatkan sebahagian besar diantaramu, Maka apakah kamu tidak memikirkan?.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد أضل منكم جبلا» خلقا جمع جبيل كقديم، وفي قراءة بضم الباء «كثيرا أفلم تكونوا تعقلون» عداوته وإضلاله أو ما حل بهم من العذاب فتؤمنون، ويقال لهم في الآخرة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَضَلَّ مِنكُمۡ جِبِلّٗا كَثِيرًاۖ أَفَلَمۡ تَكُونُواْ تَعۡقِلُونَ (جِبِلًّا: خَلْقًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya setan itu telah menyesatkan sebagian besar di antara kalian) lafal Jibillan adalah bentuk jamak dari Jabiilun seperti wazan Qadiimun, artinya makhluk. Menurut qiraat yang lain dibaca Jibullan dengan harakat Dhammah pada huruf Ba. (Maka apakah kalian tidak memikirkan?) tentang permusuhan setan dan penyesatannya; atau azab yang bakal menimpa mereka, yang karenanya mereka lalu mau beriman. Dikatakan kepada mereka di akhirat nanti

ar: هَـٰذِهِۦ جَهَنَّمُ ٱلَّتِی كُنتُمۡ تُوعَدُونَ
ar_simple: هذه جهنم ٱلتی كنتم توعدون

t.sahih-int: This is the Hellfire which you were promised
t.asad: “This, then, is the hell of which you were warned again and again
t.yusuf-ali: This is the Hell of which ye were (repeatedly) warned
t.pickthall: This is hell which ye were promised (if ye followed him)
t.arberry: This is Gehenna, then, the same that you were promised
t.hilali-khan: This is Hell which you were promised
t.itani: This is Hellfire, which you were promised.
t.mubarakpuri: This is Hell which you were promised!
t.qarib: This is the Hell that you were promised
t.wahiduddin: This is the Hell you were promised
t.shakir: This is the hell with which you were threatened
t.haleem: So this is the Fire that you were warned against
t.maariful: (Now) this is the Jahannam of which you were consistently warned
t.khattab: This is the Hell you were warned of
t.ghali: This is Hell, which you were promised
t.irving: This is Hell, which you were threatened with
t.iwq: This is the Hellfire which you were promised

t_id.kemenag: Inilah Jahannam yang dahulu kamu diancam (dengannya).
t_id.sabiq: Kepada mereka dikatakan, "Inilah neraka jahanam yang telah dijanjikan saat kalian berada di dunia. Ia merupakan balasan atas kekufuran kalian.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Inilah Jahanam yang kalian dahulu diancam) dengannya.
t_id.iwq: Inilah neraka yang dahulu kamu diancam (dengannya).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «هذه جهنم التي كنتم توعدون» بها
tafsir.siraj-ar: هَٰذِهِۦ جَهَنَّمُ ٱلَّتِي كُنتُمۡ تُوعَدُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Inilah Jahanam yang kalian dahulu diancam) dengannya

ar: ٱصۡلَوۡهَا ٱلۡیَوۡمَ بِمَا كُنتُمۡ تَكۡفُرُونَ
ar_simple: ٱصلوها ٱلیوم بما كنتم تكفرون

t.sahih-int: [Enter to] burn therein today for what you used to deny
t.asad: endure it today as an outcome of your persistent denial of the truth!”
t.yusuf-ali: Embrace ye the (fire) this Day, for that ye (persistently) rejected (Truth)
t.pickthall: Burn therein this day for that ye disbelieved
t.arberry: roast well in it today, for that you were unbelievers
t.hilali-khan: Burn therein this Day, for that you used to disbelieve
t.itani: Roast in it today, because you persistently disbelieved.
t.mubarakpuri: Burn (enter) therein this Day, for that you used to disbelieve.
t.qarib: Enter it today as a result of your having denied the truth
t.wahiduddin: Enter it this Day on account of your denial of the truth
t.shakir: Enter into it this day because you disbelieved
t.haleem: Enter it today, because you went on ignoring [my commands].’
t.maariful: Enter it today, because you have been persistently denying (the truth).”
t.khattab: Burn in it Today for your disbelief.”
t.ghali: Roast in it today, for that you disbelieved
t.irving: Roast in it today because you have disbelieved
t.iwq: Enter to burn therein today for what you used to deny

t_id.kemenag: Masuklah ke dalamnya pada hari ini disebabkan kamu dahulu mengingkarinya.
t_id.sabiq: Masuklah kalian ke dalam neraka jahanam ini. Pada hari ini, panas neraka jahanam itu semakin keras dengan kekufuran kalian."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Masuklah ke dalamnya pada hari ini disebabkan kalian dahulu mengingkarinya.)
t_id.iwq: Masuklah ke dalamnya pada hari ini disebabkan kamu dahulu mengingkarinya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «اصلوْها اليوم بما كنتم تكفرون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱصۡلَوۡهَا ٱلۡيَوۡمَ بِمَا كُنتُمۡ تَكۡفُرُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Masuklah ke dalamnya pada hari ini disebabkan kalian dahulu mengingkarinya

ar: ٱلۡیَوۡمَ نَخۡتِمُ عَلَىٰۤ أَفۡوَ ٰهِهِمۡ وَتُكَلِّمُنَاۤ أَیۡدِیهِمۡ وَتَشۡهَدُ أَرۡجُلُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ یَكۡسِبُونَ
ar_simple: ٱلیوم نختم على أفو ههم وتكلمنا أیدیهم وتشهد أرجلهم بما كانوا یكسبون

t.sahih-int: That Day, We will seal over their mouths, and their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify about what they used to earn
t.asad: On that Day We shall set a seal on their mouths but their hands will speak unto Us, and their feet will bear witness to whatever they have earned [in life]
t.yusuf-ali: That Day shall We set a seal on their mouths. But their hands will speak to us, and their feet bear witness, to all that they did
t.pickthall: This day We seal up their mouths, and their hands speak out to Us and their feet bear witness as to what they used to earn
t.arberry: Today We set a seal on their mouths, and their hands speak to Us, and their feet bear witness as to what they have been earning
t.hilali-khan: This Day, We shall seal up their mouths, and their hands will speak to Us, and their legs will bear witness to what they used to earn. (It is said that one's left thigh will be the first to bear the witness). [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. 22, Page]
t.itani: On this Day, We will seal their mouths, and their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify to everything they had done.
t.mubarakpuri: This Day, We shall seal up their mouths, and their hands will speak to Us, and their legs will bear witness to what they used to earn.
t.qarib: Today, We will seal over their mouths, but their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify about what they used to do
t.wahiduddin: Today We shall seal up their mouths and their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will bear witness to their misdeeds
t.shakir: On that day We will set a seal upon their mouths, and their hands shall speak to Us, and their feet shall bear witness of what they earned
t.haleem: On that Day We shall seal up their mouths, but their hands will speak to Us, and their feet bear witness to everything they have done
t.maariful: Today We will set a seal on their mouths, and their hands will speak to Us, and their legs will bear witness about what they used to do
t.khattab: On this Day We will seal their mouths, their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify to what they used to commit
t.ghali: Today We seal up their mouths, and their hands speak to Us, and their legs testify to what they have been earning
t.irving: That day We shall seal their mouths up while their hands will speak to Us and their feet bear witness about what they have been earning
t.iwq: That Day, We will seal over their mouths, and their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify about what they used to earn

t_id.kemenag: Pada hari ini Kami tutup mulut mereka; dan berkatalah kepada Kami tangan mereka dan memberi kesaksianlah kaki mereka terhadap apa yang dahulu mereka usahakan.
t_id.sabiq: Pada hari itu Kami tutup mulut mereka sehingga tidak dapat berkata. Tangan-tangan merekalah yang berbicara kepada Kami. Dan kaki-kaki mereka pun berbicara sebagai saksi atas perbuatan-perbuatan mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Pada hari ini Kami tutup mulut mereka) mulut orang-orang kafir, karena mereka mengatakan, yaitu sebagaimana yang disitir oleh firman-Nya, "Demi Allah, Rabb kami, tiadalah kami mempersekutukan Allah." (Q.S. 6 Al An'am, 23) (Dan berkatalah kepada Kami tangan mereka dan kaki mereka memberi kesaksian) juga anggota-anggota mereka lainnya (terhadap apa yang dahulu mereka kerjakan) setiap anggota tubuh mengucapkan apa yang telah diperbuatnya.
t_id.iwq: Pada hari ini Kami tutup mulut mereka; dan berkatalah kepada Kami tangan mereka dan memberi kesaksianlah kaki mereka terhadap apa yang dahulu mereka usahakan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (اليوم نختم على أفواههم) أي الكفار لقولهم "والله ربنا ما كنا مشركين" (وتكلمنا أيديهم وتشهد أرجلهم) وغيرها (بما كانوا يكسبون) فكل عضو ينطق بما صدر منه
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱلۡيَوۡمَ نَخۡتِمُ عَلَىٰٓ أَفۡوَٰهِهِمۡ وَتُكَلِّمُنَآ أَيۡدِيهِمۡ وَتَشۡهَدُ أَرۡجُلُهُم بِمَا كَانُواْ يَكۡسِبُونَ (نَخْتِمُ: نَطْبَعُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pada hari ini Kami tutup mulut mereka) mulut orang-orang kafir, karena mereka mengatakan, yaitu sebagaimana yang disitir oleh firman-Nya, "Demi Allah, Rabb kami, tiadalah kami mempersekutukan Allah." (Q.S. 6 Al An'am, 23) (Dan berkatalah kepada Kami tangan mereka dan kaki mereka memberi kesaksian) juga anggota-anggota mereka lainnya (terhadap apa yang dahulu mereka kerjakan) setiap anggota tubuh mengucapkan apa yang telah diperbuatnya

ar: وَلَوۡ نَشَاۤءُ لَطَمَسۡنَا عَلَىٰۤ أَعۡیُنِهِمۡ فَٱسۡتَبَقُوا۟ ٱلصِّرَ ٰطَ فَأَنَّىٰ یُبۡصِرُونَ
ar_simple: ولو نشاء لطمسنا على أعینهم فٱستبقوا ٱلصر ط فأنى یبصرون

t.sahih-int: And if We willed, We could have obliterated their eyes, and they would race to [find] the path, and how could they see
t.asad: NOW HAD IT BEEN Our will [that men should not be able to discern between right and wrong], We could surely have deprived them of their sight, so that they would stray forever from the [right] way: for how could they have had insight [into what is true]
t.yusuf-ali: If it had been our Will, We could surely have blotted out their eyes; then should they have run about groping for the Path, but how could they have seen
t.pickthall: And had We willed, We verily could have quenched their eyesight so that they should struggle for the way. Then how could they have seen
t.arberry: Did We will, We would have obliterated their eyes, then they would race to the path, but how would they see
t.hilali-khan: And if it had been Our Will, We would surely have wiped out (blinded) their eyes, so that they would struggle for the Path, how then would they see
t.itani: If We will, We can blind their eyes as they rush towards the path—but how will they see?
t.mubarakpuri: And if it had been Our will, We would surely have wiped out their eyes, so that they would struggle for the path, how then would they see
t.qarib: And if We willed it, We could have taken away their sight, and they would have struggled to [find] the path, but how could they have seen it
t.wahiduddin: If it had been Our will, We could have put out their eyes. They would have struggled to find the way, but how could they have seen it
t.shakir: And if We please We would certainly put out their eyes, then they would run about groping for the way, but how should they see
t.haleem: If it had been Our will, We could have taken away their sight. They would have struggled to find the way, but how could they have seen it
t.maariful: If We so will, We would wipe out their eyes (right here in this world), and they would be racing towards the way, but how would they see
t.khattab: Had We willed, We could have easily blinded their eyes, so they would struggle to find their way. How then could they see
t.ghali: And if We had (so) decided, We would indeed have obliterated their eyes. Then they would race with each other to the Path, yet however would they behold (it)
t.irving: If We so wished, We would put their eyes out so they must grope along the Road. Yet how will they ever notice [anything]
t.iwq: And if We willed, We could have obliterated their eyes, and they would race to find the path, and how could they see

t_id.kemenag: Dan jikalau Kami menghendaki pastilah Kami hapuskan penglihatan mata mereka; lalu mereka berlomba-lomba (mencari) jalan, Maka betapakah mereka dapat melihat(nya).
t_id.sabiq: Dan jika Kami menghendaki untuk menghilangkan penglihatan mereka di dunia, niscaya Kami akan butakan mereka. Lalu mereka berlomba-lomba meniti jalan yang harus mereka lalui. Tapi mereka tidak dapat lagi melihat jalan itu. Bagaimana mungkin mereka dapat melihatnya, bukankah Kami telah membuat mereka buta?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan jika Kami menghendaki pastilah Kami hapuskan penglihatan mereka) Kami jadikan penglihatan mereka buta sama sekali (lalu mereka berlomba-lomba) bersegera (-mencari- jalan) untuk pergi sebagaimana kebiasaan mereka. (Maka betapakah) bagaimanakah (mereka dapat melihat) jalan itu, jika mereka dalam keadaan buta? Yakni mereka pasti tidak akan dapat melihat jalan itu.
t_id.iwq: Dan jikalau Kami menghendaki pastilah Kami hapuskan penglihatan mata mereka; lalu mereka berlomba-lomba (mencari) jalan, Maka betapakah mereka dapat melihat(nya).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولو نشاء لطمسنا على أعينهم» لأعميناها طمسا «فاستَبَقُوا» ابتدروا «الصراط» الطريق ذاهبين كعادتهم «فأنَّا» فكيف «يبصرون» حينئذ؟ أي لا يبصرون
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَوۡ نَشَآءُ لَطَمَسۡنَا عَلَىٰٓ أَعۡيُنِهِمۡ فَٱسۡتَبَقُواْ ٱلصِّرَٰطَ فَأَنَّىٰ يُبۡصِرُونَ (فَاسْتَبَقُوا الصِّرَاطَ: بَادَرُوا إِلَى الطَّرِيقِ؛ لِيَجْتَازُوهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan jika Kami menghendaki pastilah Kami hapuskan penglihatan mereka) Kami jadikan penglihatan mereka buta sama sekali (lalu mereka berlomba-lomba) bersegera (-mencari- jalan) untuk pergi sebagaimana kebiasaan mereka. (Maka betapakah) bagaimanakah (mereka dapat melihat) jalan itu, jika mereka dalam keadaan buta? Yakni mereka pasti tidak akan dapat melihat jalan itu

ar: وَلَوۡ نَشَاۤءُ لَمَسَخۡنَـٰهُمۡ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِهِمۡ فَمَا ٱسۡتَطَـٰعُوا۟ مُضِیࣰّا وَلَا یَرۡجِعُونَ
ar_simple: ولو نشاء لمسخنهم على مكانتهم فما ٱستطعوا مضیا ولا یرجعون

t.sahih-int: And if We willed, We could have deformed them, [paralyzing them] in their places so they would not be able to proceed, nor could they return
t.asad: And had it been Our will [that they should not be free to choose between right and wrong], We could surely have given them a different nature [and created them as beings rooted] in their places, so that they would not be able to move forward, and could not turn back
t.yusuf-ali: And if it had been Our Will, We could have transformed them (to remain) in their places; then should they have been unable to move about, nor could they have returned (after error)
t.pickthall: And had We willed, We verily could have fixed them in their place, making them powerless to go forward or turn back
t.arberry: Did We will, We would have changed them where they were, then they could not go on, nor could they return
t.hilali-khan: And if it had been Our Will, We could have transformed them (into animals or lifeless objects) in their places. Then they should have been unable to go forward (move about) nor they could have turned back. [As it happened with the Jews see Verse 7:166 The Quran]
t.itani: And if We will, We can cripple them in their place; so they can neither move forward, nor go back.
t.mubarakpuri: And if it had been Our will, We could have transformed them in their places. Then they would have been unable to go forward (move about) nor they could have turned back.
t.qarib: And if We had so willed, We could have crippled them where they stood, so that they could move neither forward nor backward
t.wahiduddin: If it had been Our will, We could have paralysed them where they stood, so that they would not be able to go forward or turn back
t.shakir: And if We please We would surely transform them in their place, then they would not be able to go on, nor will they return
t.haleem: If it had been Our will, We could have paralysed them where they stood, so that they could not move forward or backward
t.maariful: And If We so will, We would disfigure them at their places, and they would not be able to move, nor would they return
t.khattab: And had We willed, We could have transfigured them on the spot, so they could neither progress forward nor turn back
t.ghali: And if We had (so) decided, We would have indeed disfigured them in (spite of) their situation; then in no way could they be passing, nor could they return
t.irving: If We wished, We would nail them to the spot so they would not manage to keep on going nor would they ever return
t.iwq: And if We willed, We could have deformed them, paralyzing them in their places so they would not be able to proceed, nor could they return

t_id.kemenag: Dan jikalau Kami menghendaki pastilah Kami ubah mereka di tempat mereka berada; maka mereka tidak sanggup berjalan lagi dan tidak (pula) sanggup kembali.
t_id.sabiq: Dan jikalau Kami menghendaki untuk mengubah bentuk mereka, niscaya Kami akan mengubah mereka ke dalam bentuk yang buruk, menggantikan kekuatan dan kedudukan yang sebelumnya mereka miliki. Maka mereka pun tidak dapat berjalan ke depan atau mundur ke belakang kareka Kami telah menghilangkan kekuatan yang ada pada mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan jika Kami menghendaki pastilah Kami ubah mereka) diubah menjadi kera, babi atau batu (di tempat mereka berada) menurut qiraat yang lain lafal Makanatihim dibaca dalam bentuk jamak, yaitu Makaanaatihim, yaitu di tempat-tempat mereka (maka mereka tidak sanggup berjalan dan tidak pula sanggup kembali) yakni mereka tidak dapat pergi dan tidak dapat pulang kembali.
t_id.iwq: Dan jikalau Kami menghendaki pastilah Kami ubah mereka di tempat mereka berada; maka mereka tidak sanggup berjalan lagi dan tidak (pula) sanggup kembali.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولو نشاء لمسخناهم» قردة وخنازير أو حجارة «على مكانتهم» وفي قراءة مكاناتهم جمع مكانة بمعنى مكان أي في منازلهم «فما استطاعوا مضيا ولا يرجعون» أي لم يقدروا على ذهاب ولا مجيء
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَوۡ نَشَآءُ لَمَسَخۡنَٰهُمۡ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِهِمۡ فَمَا ٱسۡتَطَٰعُواْ مُضِيّٗا وَلَا يَرۡجِعُونَ (لَمَسَخْنَاهُمْ: لَغَيَّرْنَا خَلْقَهُمْ, مَكَانَتِهِمْ: أَمَاكِنِهِمْ, مُضِيًّا: أَنْ يَمْضُوا أَمَامَهُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan jika Kami menghendaki pastilah Kami ubah mereka) diubah menjadi kera, babi atau batu (di tempat mereka berada) menurut qiraat yang lain lafal Makanatihim dibaca dalam bentuk jamak, yaitu Makaanaatihim, yaitu di tempat-tempat mereka (maka mereka tidak sanggup berjalan dan tidak pula sanggup kembali) yakni mereka tidak dapat pergi dan tidak dapat pulang kembali

ar: وَمَن نُّعَمِّرۡهُ نُنَكِّسۡهُ فِی ٱلۡخَلۡقِۚ أَفَلَا یَعۡقِلُونَ
ar_simple: ومن نعمره ننكسه فی ٱلخلق أفلا یعقلون

t.sahih-int: And he to whom We grant long life We reverse in creation; so will they not understand
t.asad: But [let them always remember that] if We lengthen a human being’s days, We also cause him to decline in his powers [when he grows old]: will they not, then, use their reason
t.yusuf-ali: If We grant long life to any, We cause him to be reversed in nature: Will they not then understand
t.pickthall: He whom we bring unto old age, We reverse him in creation (making him go back to weakness after strength). Have ye then no sense
t.arberry: And to whomsoever We give long life, We bend him over in His constitution; what, do they not understand
t.hilali-khan: And he whom We grant long life, We reverse him in creation (weakness after strength). Will they not then understand
t.itani: Whomever We grant old age, We reverse his development. Do they not understand?
t.mubarakpuri: And he whom We grant long life -- We reverse him in creation. Will they not then understand
t.qarib: When We grant a person long life, We also cause him to decline in power, so will they not understand
t.wahiduddin: If We extend anyone's life, We reverse his development. Can they not use their reason
t.shakir: And whomsoever We cause to live long, We reduce (him) to an abject state in constitution; do they not then understand
t.haleem: If We extend anyone’s life, We reverse his development. Do they not use their reason
t.maariful: And whomsoever We give long life, we reverse him in creation. Then, do they have no sense
t.khattab: And whoever We grant a long life, We reverse them in development. Will they not then understand
t.ghali: And to whomever We give long life, We cause him to relapse in creation. Do they then not consider
t.irving: Anyone We grant long life to, We switch around within creation. Will they not use their reason
t.iwq: And he to whom We grant long life We reverse in creation; so will they not understand

t_id.kemenag: Dan barangsiapa yang Kami panjangkan umurnya niscaya Kami kembalikan dia kepada kejadian(nya). Maka apakah mereka tidak memikirkan?
t_id.sabiq: Barangsiapa yang Kami panjangkan usianya, Kami akan mengembalikannya dari kuat menjadi lemah. Tidakkah mereka berpikir akan kekuasaan Kami melakukan hal itu sehingga mereka menyadari bahwa dunia ini hanyalah ediaman yang fana dan akhirat merupakan tempat yang abadi?(1). (1) Hal itu terjadi karena kehidupan manusia mengalami tiga fase, yaitu fase pertumbuhan, fase kematangan dan fase atrofi atau penyusutan. Seseorang akan memasuki masa tua ketika pada dirinya mulai terjadi penyusutan parenkim di ginjal, jantung, kelenjar gondok dan pankreas. Itu semua mempunyai peranan dalam membuat seluruh tubuh menjadi lemah. Pembuluh nadi pun, saat itu, mulai mengalami pengerasan dan penyusutan. Dengan demikian, darah yang mengalir ke seluruh bagian tubuh pun berkurang. Akibatnya tubuh menjadi semakin lemah. Di antara penyebab ketuaan lainnya adalah bahwa daya perusak lebih kuat daripada daya pembangun (metabolisme) tubuh. Hal itu dimungkinkan karena semua sel pada tubuh mengalami perubahan terus menerus, kecuali sel otak dan urat saraf tulang belakang yang tidak pernah mengalami perubahan sepanjang hidup. Jika jumlah sel baru sama dengan jumlah sel yang mati, maka tidak akan terjadi apa-apa pada tubuh. Tetapi jika jumlah sel yang mati lebih banyak daripada sel baru pada bagian tubuh mana saja, maka bagian tubuh itu akan mengalami penyusutan. Atas dasar itu, semakin bertambah usia seseorang, jumlah selnya yang mati pun bertambah banyak. Pada gilirannya akan terjadi penambahan degradasi sel yang mengakibatkan penyusutan secara umum. Jumlah pertambahan sel-sel baru itu sendiri berbeda dari satu jaringan ke jaringan lain.
